prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, with Mulliken partial charges via PySCF. Geometry was the user-supplied .xyz (held fixed, not re-optimized). Finite-difference Fukui indices were computed over the N, N+1, and N−1 electron systems (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2).

Headline result — most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.70839130. Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742464. Dual descriptor on C = +0.59594709 (strongly electrophilic). All integrity checks passed; sum(f⁺) = sum(f⁻) = 1.0000; trustworthy: true. No warnings reported by the engine. 

Method: DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF), gas phase, Mulliken charges. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard [input.xyz]. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-175450_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
